feed.title: 8holic
feed.subtitle: The main hub by 8holic (Real name Bryan) to create a hub to link all existing projects and files.

entry.title: I got a 3D Printer!
entry.content:
Background

I have always been interested in 3D Printing, I was first introduced to 3D Printing by some external instructor where we design our pencil holder. That guy still owes me my pencil holder with almost 10 years of debt.

I was researching what 3D Printer I should get and I asked around a bit, I end up getting a Bambu P1S with an AMS 2 pro. This is because the printer I am famillar with is also of Bambu brand. Additionally I heard good reviews regarding it.

Right off the bat I was able to get the printer up and printing in about 15 minutes which suprises me. I was able to get a benchy up in no time.

In the next few days, I plan on printing a shoe rack, a laptop holder and a ship model in that order. Hope all goes well!
entry.summary: Background I have always been interested in 3D Printing, I was first introduced to 3D Printing by some external instructor where we design our pencil holder. That guy still owes me my pencil holder with almost 10 years of debt.

entry.title: Toastmaster NEW TERM
entry.content:
After a year of being Club President, it is finally over.

Club President Bryan

This was a most challanging journey, I was extremely reluctant to take up the role as I am aware I will be away from August to January. However, the circumstances forced me into it. This is how I became the club president.

Managing the club overseas

While in Spain, I continue to hold exco meeting with my club. I am glad to have some reliable lads who are able to update me on the suitation. However, my lack of physical presence is indeed an issue. During this period we are able to hold the club in stagnation, ensuring it stays as per usual. I took the role to try to mentor the Vice President of Education…but he just dipped on me halfway. I also took the role to contact people and ensure the club program sheet is well planned so the people on the ground have an easier job.

#Managing the club over here

When I return in January, the first massive issue is that the club home grounds is closed for rennovation. I had to come up with some creative solutions in order to maintain things. The first time we did was zoom meetings, however I made a crucial mistake and delayed the January meeting as there is a lack of people interested. This mistake resulted in the momentum of the club halting, and I had to work hard by talking to people, coming up with intresting themes in order to revive the momentum.

For several months we will continue online meetings, the limitation of online meetings make engagement challanging.
entry.summary: After a year of being Club President, it is finally over.

entry.title: Reading: Hoshi Ori Yume Mirai - Common Route

link.title: Reading: Hoshi Ori Yume Mirai - Common Route

entry.content:
Background

It has dawn about me that I have forgotten the common route that connects all the paths, so I thought I should cover it first.

General Thoughts

I will first talk about the first half which is introducing everyone, and the second half which is used to establish the story.

One of the most suprising part is that the protagonist,Ryosuke Hino…has hope and dreams. I know very high bar to clear, however the sheer amount that is just a nice guy blank slate or a backstory that serve to justify them being overpowered means that this actually make the protagonist intresting. We see the POV character is interested in Architecture, and this actually drives some of his actions later on. Most importantly, this means that I can actually see myself falling for him.

Afterwards we are introduced to the characters basically in this order,i think. Sora Ousaka is the shy, quiet lass who love astrology. Natsuki Segawa is a energetic lass who love photography. Azuma Morio is a figure of admiration and mature despite being airheaded. Marika Shinozaki is the sweet childhood friend who recovered from an illness. Nirasawa Shuuichi who is the a childhood friend with a friendly fierce competitive streak with the protagonist. Rikka Narusawa is cold and stoic while being good at the piano. Misa Okihara is a knowledgable and sisterly lass about marine life. Touko Yukimura who is a bit of a rebel. I felt that the introductions did a good job at establishing who everyone is and the type of interest and personality they have. So I have no issue with the first segment at all.

For the second segment we are introduced to the Tanabata festival, and the town unique celeberation. This set up the core driving force for most of the actions taken in the school segment.

For this segment I felt that it was allright, it did its job to make the introduced character into a group. Narusawa-san is the only one that felt like she is literally dragged there by the party. This segment was not the most intresting since they still include more information for every character, and at this point I felt like the elaboration isn’t really necessary. This entire segment is basically the “checkbox” to decide whose route you are romancing.

My biggest issue is that the choices does not feel like they matter. Early on Shinozaki-san has a choice to wait for her or go home, regardless of what you choose you still wait for her. It is probably used to count which part to put the player on but why not just…skip it. As a result I wasn’t aware that these choices affect who we are pursuing later(since they outcome is exactly the same). I think it will be fine to skip it, so if we don’t wait for Marika we skip the entire segment, which makes more sense into the romance part since well we spend less time with her.

The group scenes however are great, and the conversation at the end paints a good picture of what to expect from each girl when pursuing them. All the silly games they play does help in creating the atmosphere.

Summary for the common route

I like the establishment of all the characters, as well as the story. I will appericate if the game is not in fear that I will miss their brilliant writing.

Sora Route Natsuki Route
entry.summary: Background It has dawn about me that I have forgotten the common route that connects all the paths, so I thought I should cover it first.

entry.title: Reading: Hoshi Ori Yume Mirai - Natuuki Route

link.title: Reading: Hoshi Ori Yume Mirai - Natuuki Route

entry.content:
Background

The reason I decide to attempt to romance her is simply because she is the most prominent of all the love interest in Ousaka-san path. From aiding in the love confession to showing up in the after story.

General Thoughts - School Arc

Segawa-san School Arc difference is that she basically dragged the protagonist along and they eventurally developed feelings. Her primary theme is photography. Her route is devoid of any major conflict and basically sees the protagonist keeping up with her as she take photos for the club. Notably what the astrology and nature club end up doing both end up changing to make her a major player.

The love confession felt kinda abrupt. After spending enough time with each other, they realize they like one another. Or at least the protagonist seems to think so, however his obviliousness doesnt exactly convince me so. It does make use of photography that I find was neat,and is a very Segawa way of confessing.

It primary focus on her obession with photography and the various things they did. The ending felt a little abrupt as they went to the festival and it ends…just like that I am a little sad that the group trip they were talking about just didn’t happen. It might be better if that was never mentioned since I was expecting, you know that group trip to be the climax of the story.

General Thoughts - After Story

Natsuki(From here on) After Story place a heavy focus on the post marriage life, with a heavy emphasis on the ideal post marriage life. It is a very fluffy read and seems to cover the honeymoon period. The ending fits Natsuki Hino quite well and is photography relevant. They have their rituals, the people around them treating them after the marriage, and how they perform the chores and cook together when they are living as a couple.The story ends with the actual ceremony for the marriage conducted by the school

General Thoughts - The Sex Scene

She has around 9 scenes I believe, and somehow all of them are plot relevant as for a lack of a better words showing them fucking raw with zero damn about the world leading to their shotgun marriage is pherhaps the most realistic part about fucking like rabbits. Even if they discover they have no child it is highly belivable. The most plot relevant one is when they are both late due to fucking deep at night. The rest is passable by explaining how they can believe in the pregency.

General Thoughts - Overall

Segawa route is rather unique due to her airheadness, and unique personality. It is a fluffy story that somehow doesn’t stick in my mind. I am optimistic that she might not be my type.
entry.summary: Background The reason I decide to attempt to romance her is simply because she is the most prominent of all the love interest in Ousaka-san path. From aiding in the love confession to showing up in the after story.

entry.title: Settler Frontier, Coding Workshop
entry.content:
This is the poster of the event.

The initial motivation for doing the workshop is simply to test the water if more technical workshop can do well in the community. As someone who has gotten interested in software, I have went through a module on computational thinking. After witnessing my classmate solution which involves several hundred primitive statement to get things done, I reazlie that it is not as intutive as I thought

Play Frontier Explorer

The game is designed with programming background in mind, it involves a maze where they have to navigate. I was able to make use of AI to convert my python syntax and logic into javascript, the primary challange is to figure out how to structure the syntax such that it is easier to expand. Overall I made the choice to make it convert into a python script in the back. It is intentionally coded such that it is possible for both semantic error and syntax error to occur, just like real programming.

Using my experience from modding Stellaris, I decide I do not want to write code in order to add more stages. As a result I make it read a JSON file outside, so I can add stages easier.

Additioanlly the instructions are read naturally then moved by some relatively else if logic.

I tried my hand at another game, that actually uses Settler Frontier theme accurately which involve the player actually going around and trying to survvie. Unfortuatnely I was unable to finish it on time. You can check it out below.

Play Settler Frontier
entry.summary: This is the poster of the event.

entry.title: Reading: Hoshi Ori Yume Mirai - Sora Route

link.title: Reading: Hoshi Ori Yume Mirai - Sora Route

entry.content:
General Thoughts - School Arc

So in this route we are basically stuck with Sora and assisting with the astrology club, who become talkative when it is about the stars. Notably Sora does demonstrate some intresting knowledge about astrology and the associated mythology. There are some light drama and issues they faced which are solved almost immeditely, however the brief moment of setback are realsitic and truely feel like life and dead for that age.

The love confession and realization has quite a bit of build up, from when our protagonist realizes his feelings, to learning more about her then finally confessing at a chance. Notably the protagonist does not actually get a reply immedietly and has to wait for her response. They continue per normal until she resond in affimartive. The group of friends meanwhile play a supporting role offsetting Sora shy nature, and continue to play a role through the festival contribution.

The next part can be broken down into two segment, but generally they face some problem and overcome them. The best way to describe it is that everyone work hard and it eventurally worked out. It felt like they really cared for each other. Notably I was actually suprised at the third setback(since it has been so drama light). The finale felt like a suitable conclusion as we see their hard work pay off and Sora dream is realized.

General Thoughts - After Story

Whereas the school arc mainly focus on realizing Sora dream, there is a notably swap here where the protagonist take center stage as he open his own firm with Sora and best boss Azuma supporting him. Sora at this point already mostly accomplished her goals in astrology which the protagonist get to witness, the protagonist meanwhile face some struggles maintaining his work life. My favorite scene is when the protagonist faints from overwork, and immeditely continue trying to work again until Sora propose to him. Where the terms she proposal shows she is willing to give up her own dream just to support him,causing the protagonist to realize how he has screwed up and take action to correct.. Eventurally he was able to fufill his own dreams, and then he finally marry Sora and gaze the stars together. The post credit scene confirms that Sora dreams is well and alive after the marriage as he and the other friends arrive to support Sora.

General Thoughts - The Sex Scene

She has around 10 scene I believe, and most of the scene felt extremely simillar. Basically I think the plot relevant ones are probably exactly 2. The first is used to show her more assertive side, and the second is used to show how she is willing to accept his fetishes as a part of him. Some of them does depict her as being more careful, however the usage of condom dies after the first raw experience so I am not sure if the above was intended.

General Thoughts - Overall

This is a strong introduction to the novel, I like that it is relatively low stakes and used to further the characters. It has a strong element of mutually supportive relatioship, and it helps Sora is really cute throughout the route.
entry.summary: General Thoughts - School Arc So in this route we are basically stuck with Sora and assisting with the astrology club, who become talkative when it is about the stars. Notably Sora does demonstrate some intresting knowledge about astrology and the associated mythology. There are some light drama and issues they faced which are solved almost immeditely, however the brief moment of setback are realsitic and truely feel like life and dead for that age.

entry.title: Reading: Atomic Habits
entry.content:
Background

I was reading the book Atomic Habits and I was reading the first few pages. I found what was said to be insightful, in determining what I want to be instead of the end goal. I find that usually when my parents ask me to clean my room usually it get messy again soon enough. Reading this made me realize that it is purely because the habits do not substain the goal. So I wish to reflect on this. Starting with the below

What type of person do I want to be?

This question is suprising difficult to answer, despite being a seemingly easy question. I broke it down into the following questions to answer instead.

What am I doing NOW

-Going to internship -Preparing for University -Play video games -Running my Toastmaster Club -Helping my community network in tech areas -Mindlessly browsing and listening to Music -Cleaning up my room

What will I CONTINUE doing/STOP doing?

What I want to do now What I will stop doing
Studying OSINT/tech related things Going to University

I was a little suprise to see that I listed both studying and University. I listed the two and I think I see the issue

Aspect University Education Self Study (OSINT/tech)
Pace Fixed, often rushed Self‑paced, can slow down to understand
Teaching style Professors focused on research, not teaching Self‑selected resources, learn by doing
Motivation Studying for tests, external pressure Internal curiosity, goals I set myself
Goal setting Curriculum‑driven, rigid I set the study goals and path
Schedule control Fixed class times, attendance requirements I decide when, how long, and the rhythm
Autonomy over content Core subjects, prerequisites, limited choice I choose exactly what I want, in any order
Evaluation pressure Grades, exams, GPA, bell curve Self‑assessment, pulbic perception
Hierarchy Professors as authority, power distance Bad resources get discarded
Intellectual freedom Curriculum decides what’s relevant I get to chase after useless knowledge

I did not realize these restriction until I head to University Charles III of Madrid for an exchange semester, where the way lessosn are conducted there affected my thoughts. The key difference is the the professors are more open to questions and meeting with students, as well as more intellectual freedom to explore topics we are interested in(even if my group squandered it). I think this is why it is more toleratable even through it is signficantly more difficult. I want to solve the ‘how’ and not go through a rigid process.

Based on this, here is my first identity: I want to be a student of technology.

What I want to do now What I will stop doing
Travel overseas Mindlessly Browse the web
Do things with intent Lose time to Listening to Music
Finish reading my bookshelf Stop buying more books
Help uplift the community Eat unhealth sugarly drinks
Go for Health Checkup  

These are group together because they have a common theme, which is active vs passive consumption. The left side are about doing and experiencing things, while most of the thing I want to stop are passive consumptions. For things on the left I felt that they are usually harder to start, however upon starting it is harder to stop. Whereas things on the right are easy to start and easy to stay engaged, but at the end I felt that I haven’t done anything.

So the second Identity will be: I want to be someone who does and experience.
entry.summary: Background I was reading the book Atomic Habits and I was reading the first few pages. I found what was said to be insightful, in determining what I want to be instead of the end goal. I find that usually when my parents ask me to clean my room usually it get messy again soon enough. Reading this made me realize that it is purely because the habits do not substain the goal. So I wish to reflect on this. Starting with the below What type of person do I want to be? This question is suprising difficult to answer, despite being a seemingly easy question. I broke it down into the following questions to answer instead. What am I doing NOW -Going to internship -Preparing for University -Play video games -Running my Toastmaster Club -Helping my community network in tech areas -Mindlessly browsing and listening to Music -Cleaning up my room What will I CONTINUE doing/STOP doing?

entry.title: Limbus Company ID Visualization
entry.content:
Visualization

Ideation

I heard people mention a few stats like certain character having the longest wait, so I was always kinda curious. I decide to take this own to use some of the things I learnt last semester.

getting the data point.

Since I already know there is a data point, I start off by checking robot.txt to make sure that they are ok with it. I noticed they block a lot of AI agents, and some spider. So I decide to use Selenium. The idea I had was that I would first get the URL, then I will run it through to grab certain information. Most of the challanges are things I partially remember how to deal with, such as tabs and simillar things. I set my agent to have a 1 second delay on every agent just to ensure I do not DDOS the server by accident. Overall it went pretty ok, but these are the challanges.

  1. Navigating the differnt character This one was pretty ok, I realize very early on that the ID divison goes up by 2 instead of 1 for ID, so I ran two loops, the first loop will run the internal ID, the second loop oversee the entire thing to change character. We get the url here.
  2. Inconsistent format Among the older ID, sometimes the date is just slightly enough that the XPath misses it, I think there is an extra divison somewhere because this is the difference. I end up doing a either/or for certain parameters, however this is not that significant and I corrected some of them manually. For the major one like skills, I use a fuzzy form of searching instead, searching for Skill1 is enough compared to Skill1_0
  3. All the weird characters Something intresting, but excel cried pretty badly when processing korean/japanese/chinese characters. Installed the language pack to make this work out. I also wanted to get the element affilation even through I didn’t do anything about it, after looking around I decide that I can simply grab the name of the png, which is usually used to visually tell a reader.

But all in all, the data was relatively easy to get due to the relatively standalized format, it is not super clean like the perfect data my school gave me, but it is a little challanging to get. Most of the challange come from navigating the site.

Cleaning the data was intresting as well, however I will cover this more in the next part.

Visualizing the data

I learnt a bit about google data studio, so over here I decide to try my hand. Importing the data isn’t too difficult, however I soon realize that this is the hardest segment Challanges faced

  1. Getting the X axis to be a time series Upon going in I already have a pretty good idea what I want the graph to look like, which is an issue when all the X axis generally support metrics only and not dates. Time series results in a line which isn’t exactly suited for these 1 off data. Initally I thought to use the UEIX format for date in order to display as metrics, however due to the large value, it ends up really skewing the graph making it hard to read. In the end I just used a community visuzliaer instead. Thank you supermetrics
  2. Making the data format correct It become very clear that 19.02.2026 is being registered as a text, which is an issue, this wasn’t too bad as I used power query(or the google equivalent) to convert it into a date format. It took a while to get the formula right but it was pretty ok
  3. Y axis must be a metrics By far the largest issue I spend on, since I am using the names of the character, I basically have 12 strings while they only accept numbers. I looked around a bit, and eventurally decide I should convert the names into number so I can plot the graph. This leads to the final but most vexxing issue
  4. The legends order is wrong Since the previous segment is the Y axis, and the numbers don’t exactly mean anything, we will need someway to read the data. Initally I thought since our name has numbers, we will sort via numerical order, however the order seems to be random. Initally I thought it was alphabetical order, so I ran the parameter to add a number in front of the sinner names…and nothing changed. It isn’t numerical either since when I change the number everything jumped. I think it has something to do with the sort tab but I fiddle with it and nothing change. I have no idea what was causing this and was getting a little fustrated, so I decide perfection is the enemy of good. So I just published it on reddit: https://www.reddit.com/r/limbuscompany/comments/1r8y6iv/as_limbus_enter_its_third_year_i_tried/ As of writing I literally just publish it so I am unsure of the reception. However I believe I have reach a good end point.

Conclusion

Overall I learnt a bit about data visualization and how finnicky the software can be at competiting against what I wanted. I also learnt a bit about using creative walkarounds, through the final issue is still unresolved. I also realize how scary it is to publish things I have done online even if fundamentally it doesn’t matter, I am unsure why? Maybe it is the fear of someone saying something mean about it, this would be an area of growth.

entry.title: Pre Exchange Experience-Day 1
entry.content:
Pre Exchange Experience - Italy Tourism Day 1

Background

As a student heading for exchange, I decided to do a trip in europe since I haven’t really travel outside of Asia. I decided on Italy due to its relative close proximity to my exchange university in Spain.

Milan - Standout Moments

First impressions

Upon arrival the first thing I noticed is that the buildings seems older. There isn’t lots of skyscraper, most buildings are quite old

Duomo di Milano

Milan’s Cathedral was the first location I visited, it looks pretty old. I really enjoyed looking at the various status around it, going inside I see the pillars and decorations. It is the first time I am seeing such a detailed cathedral.

The interior is very spacious. I sat down and just enjoyed the enviroment, it is very quiet.

I climbed to the rooftop,it was a tough climb. A few hundred steps, I didn’t take the elevator. The view of the city is nice, it feels like a older city.

Wandering Around

I came across this layout of the city, and it is suprising. I have never seen this before, it feels intresting to see these maps and think of why they were built in the past

The next thing I noticed is the existance of these water fountain. In Singapore these are rare and inconvenient, I cannot believe that these are just out here. I drank a bit and the water was really refreshing.

Museo del Duomo di Milano

In the museum they talk about the history of the cathedral, it is quite intesting. I saw how they built the cathedral up over time. I went to a few museum so I am just going to group them together, the above image is that of a clock. It has always just worked I have never thought about how such a simple clock works all the time.

This was a pretty creepy underground area, It was an intresting experience walking underground.

Final thoughts

The first day impression started with a robbery on the train, but overall it was pretty exciting. I felt thrusted in a foreign world, everything is new and exciting.

Through the most challanging part was when the hotel cancel on me as i board the flight, booking on the plane causes me a bit of anxiety but it is good it works out.

entry.title: URECA Project Presentation
entry.content:
Overview

Last year I took part in the Undergraduate Research Experience on Campus(URECA) Project. I was tasked with figuring our a way to port an experience to a more consumer friendly version. The photo above was my presentation during the URECA Conference. Overall this was a wonderful 1 year where I learnt a lot. I was supervised by Assoc Professor Benjamin.

I felt that the professor was wonderful, it was mostly unsupervised due to his busy schedule. This gave me a lot of opporunity to mostly figure things out by myself and decide on my course of action, with inputs from him during each presentation.

The things I done well is the following: -The ability to think creatively to solve issues -The ability to work consistenly on the project throughout the semester -The ability to present my work to the professor -The conclusion of the project involved it being presented to people in the community center after the above conference, I think it is a good showcase of the work to a wider audiance.

The things I think I can do better: -Clearer communication or more meaningful communiation to the professor, I am unsure if the update aren’t significant enough or he is busy but sometimes I am not sure how to proceed. Usually I can just figure something out, but at the end when it comes to publishing rights I am not sure if I should do it under my account or under the school since the school owns all the project -Preseting a demo, some of my presentation did not work when presenting to the professor and I believe he had some trouble to get it to work properly.

I think that some factors are out of control as I am not very sure about the school proceedings, NTU being a black box does not help either. While I was unable to publish the work, I hope that I have done enough that the Professor will get there eventurally.
entry.summary: Overview Last year I took part in the Undergraduate Research Experience on Campus(URECA) Project. I was tasked with figuring our a way to port an experience to a more consumer friendly version. The photo above was my presentation during the URECA Conference. Overall this was a wonderful 1 year where I learnt a lot. I was supervised by Assoc Professor Benjamin.
